title: PIPELINE: three gates must run before any render (they were skipped on video 1)
description:
Video 1 needed two Terry review rounds because gates existed but were not run.
1. hyperframes check . --at-transitions -> MUST return zero content_overlap/text_occluded/text_clipped. Was silently broken by a path bug (script ships in dist/commands/, loader only searches dist/). Fix: cp /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/hyperframes/dist/commands/layout-audit.browser.js /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/hyperframes/dist/ . RE-APPLY AFTER ANY HYPERFRAMES UPGRADE. A gate that fails to RUN is a FAILURE, not a footnote.
2. tools/scene-validator.py <project-root> -> must be ALL CLEAN. NOTE: its regex only matches literal tl.to|from|fromTo|set|add(...) so scene-shell helpers (kb/line/show/hide/card/countUp) are INVISIBLE to it - write key timeline anchors as raw tl.* or the gate under-reports.
3. Card-frame sampling on the ASSEMBLED master (luma>150 at each card cue) - make-citation-card reports '(matched)' on blank cards and on cards where the highlight landed on site nav.
Also: capture must strip ads/iframes/embedded video players before screenshot (a mortgage ad and a stranger's face both landed on citation cards).

title: hyperframes check layout audit was broken - path bug hid ALL text-overlap errors
description: ROOT CAUSE of Terry's 6:32/7:25/7:48 text-on-text defects. hyperframes 0.7.64 loadBrowserScript() looks in dist/ and dist/commands/ BUT __dirname2 resolves such that layout-audit.browser.js (which ships in dist/commands/) is not found -> 'Missing browser script layout-audit.browser.js' -> the ENTIRE Layout stage aborts with 'check_runtime_failure' while lint/motion/contrast still report clean. I saw that error on scene 03 early on, judged it 'a CLI helper issue, not my scene', and proceeded. That judgement was wrong: it disabled the exact check that catches overlapping text. FIX: cp /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/hyperframes/dist/commands/layout-audit.browser.js /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/hyperframes/dist/  (re-apply after any hyperframes upgrade). ALWAYS run: hyperframes check . --at-transitions (the --at-transitions flag samples tween start/end boundaries to catch transient overlaps). Add to PIPELINE as a hard pre-render gate.

title: 3 citation cards render blank despite tool reporting matched
description: V1-C3 (Front Office Sports), V4-C3 + V4-C4 (Athletech) render as blank white pages with an empty highlight box. pdftotext finds the quote (text layer present) so make-citation-card.py reports '(matched)', but no text paints in the PNG. Detect with PIL stddev<12. Likely lazy-loaded/invisible text in the playwright PDF. Fix: re-capture with longer settle + force text color, or screenshot-to-PDF instead of page.pdf().
notes: Card-capture: ALSO strip newsletter/email-signup/generic modals + role=dialog + paywall/interstitial before screenshot (Rolling Stone newsletter popup landed on V3-C9; Players Fund JS-render gave a black region on V5-C1/C2). close_reason: Fixed. Root cause: page.pdf() with screen-media emulation produced a text layer but no painted content on some sites, so the tool's quote matcher reported '(matched)' on a blank card. Fix = emulate_media('print') before pdf(). V4-C3/V4-C4 now render correctly; V1-C3 converted to typographic card. Detection rule added: PIL stddev<12 = blank render; never trust the tool's own 'matched' status.

title: Add negation-ladder + named-adversary devices to RETENTION-AND-HOOKS.md §2
description:
From competitor report 2026-07-22 (jarvis-private/reports/competitor-channel-report-2026-07-22.md). Two devices observed in the highest-outlier cold opens that our §2 does not yet name:

1. NAMED ADVERSARY IN SENTENCE ONE. Internet of Bugs opens 'They spent a long time trying to convince you that AI wasn't a bubble' — converts a topic into a conflict with sides and recruits the viewer in 2 seconds. Our hooks are strong on stakes, weak on conflict.
2. THE NEGATION LADDER. 'Not just because X, and not just because Y, but because ——.' Pre-empts the two guesses the viewer would make and withholds the third. Mechanically perfect fact-vs-meaning split per §3.
3. Also confirm §1 in the doc: IoB wins the top register at 5-11 min runtimes (1.60x at 5m, 1.42x at 8m, 1.16x at 9m). Our 8-min standard is validated by fresh data, not just inferred.

value: HyperFrames layout/check reports TRUNCATE their issue list (truncated:true, issues capped ~80). A flood of one error class HIDES other real errors. After fixing a large error class, ALWAYS re-run the gate — new genuine errors surface underneath. Verified 2026-07-17: a real chart-kicker/number collision only appeared after 40+ caption-overlap errors were cleared.

HyperFrames text-over-text / occlusion QC gate: scene-validator.py does NOT catch two-text-blocks-overlapping or text-hidden-behind-a-panel. The dedicated verifier is 'hyperframes layout <scene> --at-transitions --json' — it samples at every tween start/end boundary and reports code=text_occluded / content_overlap / text_overlap with time+selector+fixHint. ALWAYS run it (errorCount must be 0) on EVERY scene before assembling a master. Add it to the scene-build agent contract; do not rely on agents self-reporting no-overlap.

QC RULE — 5-SECOND MAX STATIC DISPLAY: No static visual state on screen for more than 5 seconds in any Byrddynasty video. Continuous motion required — text reveals, new elements entering, micro-animations, color pulses, position shifts. Applies to every scene, including hero cards and pivots. B-roll cutaways used only as transitions ≤5s, never extended. Also: ALWAYS run real frame-extraction QC (ffmpeg seek + screenshot every 3s) before declaring a master done. Inspect that no scene goes blank during VO and no scene is more text than visual. Verify thumbnail badges/labels don't bleed through (e.g. SKILLS EPISODE, CLI EPISODE). Lock for all future agents.

HYPERFRAMES MOTION (video-01, 2026-06-21): Under HyperFrames' seek-based renderer, FREE gsap.to(...) tweens do NOT render — only tweens added to the registered timeline (window.__timelines['root']=tl) play in the rendered MP4. The legacy .drift dot fields (free gsap.to) produced ZERO render motion, so scenes read as frozen (freezedetect confirmed identical frames 24s apart). Fix for static-hold/5s-rule violations: add ambient motion (large soft blurred radial brand glows, z-index behind content) animated ON tl with finite yoyo repeats sized to DURATION; add per-beat scale/position 'breath' on tl for beats whose content is too opaque for glows to show through. Verify with: ffmpeg -i render -vf freezedetect=n=-50dB:d=5 -an -f null - (must show no freeze_duration>=5). scene-validator.py now runs this gate (fixed 2026-06-21: was false-passing on top-level-duration hyperframes.json).

SHORTS BUILD GOTCHAS (9:16 HyperFrames, 2026-07-17): (1) Reference scene HTML copied into a short's folder as ref.*.html is parsed as a SECOND ROOT COMPOSITION -> lint error that short-circuits the layout pass (silent false pass). Keep refs in a _ref/ subfolder, never at the project root. (2) Burned-in caption chunks absolutely positioned at the same spot and crossfaded produce dozens of content_overlap/text_occluded errors — captions are the muted-viewing spine so this is critical. Fix: hard-swap with autoAlpha (visibility:hidden) or make the outgoing chunk fully clear ~0.02s BEFORE the next starts; never crossfade two chunks on identical pixels. (3) Intentional strikethrough-over-text needs data-layout-allow-occlusion (NOT -allow-overlap).

HyperFrames <video> elements MUST have a unique id= attribute — without it the renderer outputs the clip FROZEN (still frame) even though the scene looks fine in preview. Caught by 'hyperframes check'. ALSO: prefer 'hyperframes check <dir> --at-transitions --json' over 'hyperframes layout' for the final gate — check covers lint (missing video ids, CSS-transform-vs-GSAP conflicts) + runtime + layout + motion + contrast in one pass, errorCount must be 0. CSS transform on an element GSAP also tweens (e.g. transform:scaleX(0)) gets clobbered — seed it with tl.set(...,0) instead.

HyperFrames 'check' gate GOTCHA: the LAYOUT pass is short-circuited when LINT errors exist — it runs 0 samples and still reports layout errorCount 0. So a scene with lint errors (e.g. gsap_css_transform_conflict, media_missing_id) yields a FALSE PASS on text-overlap. ALWAYS clear lint errors FIRST, then re-run 'hyperframes check --at-transitions --json' to get a real layout result. Verified 2026-07-17: S4 reported layout-clean with lint errors present; after lint was fixed the layout pass actually ran and found 2 genuine content_overlap errors (a stamp line running through a card).
